contributor: university of nebraska-lincoln libraries, lincoln, ne
description: The Frontier PUBLISHED EVERY THURSDAY BY THE FRONTIER PRINTING OOMPANY D H CRONIN Editor The war correspondents who have been hit are no longer poking fun at Spanish marksmanship When Secretary Alger said that General Shafter was wise brave and prudent he left nothing else to be said _ President McKinleys cablegram congratulating General Shafter on what he has accomplished speaks for itself It is now clearly apparent thut the country is goiug right along in creasing in prosperity regardless of the war Blanco reports Spanish victories but does not explain how tho de feated Americans got right on top of Santiago Admiral Camabas principal ob ject appeared to be to get his fleet as far away from American warships as possible The trouble about a combination between Mr Cleveland and Mr Bryan would be in deciding which should lead The democratic party has many leadersall leading in different directionsbut no real leader either in or out of congress Foreigners may feel surprised at displays of American bravery but no American does The American is brave because he is born that way The times change and we change with them A stick full of type on the inside pages was
